title: RFE: podman-remote: support becoming root from other user (i.e. sudo)
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

I couldn't find this anywhere so forgive me if it is already implemented. I'd like to be able to access the root owned containers on a machine by leveraging an existing user that has sudo privileges. The usefulness of this is that a good number of machines don't allow the root user to log in, but an admin can access root containers via a user with sudo access. 

Is there a way to use `podman-remote` to become the root user via another user with appropriate permissions?

comments.body:
Good point, we should think about allowing it to go through sudo.
@baude WDYT?
it might be possible but not trivial. ill take the assignment

The workaround in [boot2podman](https://github.com/boot2podman/boot2podman) (as compared with boot2docker) was to allow the sudo user (i.e. the default user for ssh that has sudo rights) to login as root as well (using the same authorized_keys).

If there was another way to do it, maybe move to `sudo varlink bridge` or something, then that workaround could be avoided. But the old pypodman/podman-remote variables required user/host.

~PODMAN_USER~
~PODMAN_HOST~
~PODMAN_PORT~
~PODMAN_IDENTITY_FILE~

PODMAN_VARLINK_BRIDGE
PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS

For [minikube](https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube) we will probably just _skip_ using varlink, there are only like two commands that are needed anyway (`podman load` and `podman build`) and those can use the regular `ssh sudo podman` instead.

Everything else can use `kubectl` or `crictl` from Kubernetes, there is no immediate need to clone the `minikube docker-env` command IMHO. The only downside is that "build context" needs to be handled.

Would like to keep **that** environment CRI-agnostic, rather than just replace "docker" with "podman" ?

The commands above are just to cover some gaping functionality holes in the CRI specification.

----

EDIT: Turns out that using varlink and cloning docker-env is easier than messing around with ssh.

So for now, everything is duplicated using the `s/docker/podman/` method (well, more or less)...
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
I think this is still an interesting problem.

Now `sudo` has been implemented for both `podman-machine` and `minikube podman-env`...

This means that the VM no longer has to allow SSH access for the root user, in order to work.
It's been a while since I've played around with this. Any particular docs I should follow now to test?
@baude @jwhonce Is this possible now?  With API V2?
@baude @jwhonce , ping. I think we can support that as long as the ordinary user has permissions on the remote machine.
